key_points: Freshman housing fills up fast; late sign-ups left scrambling
Gameday weekends force dorm residents out of their own parking
RVs historically displaced residents from lots on Thursday/Friday
key_quotes: I used to live in Davenport hall. Back then, they would force the parking lots to be evacuated on Thursday or Friday afternoons so that RVs and campers could park in the lots.
Does housing fill up every year? Slotted time is this afternoon. Looks like all will be filled. How concerned should a freshman be?

key_points: Gameday and cowbell culture are the dominant social identity
Bulldog Bash moving indoors has upset longtime students significantly
Cotton District bar scene is lively; music scene exists but limited
key_quotes: I feel like with the changes to parking, tailgating, and Bash the university has swung so far away from being the more student-focused place it was when I started here 5 years ago.
This is a reoccurring theme. It always gets worse. I feel bad for the freshmen because they don't have it as good as I had it.
I miss dubstep tuesdays at daves.

key_points: Aramark runs dining and students actively want them gone
Mandatory 'Dawg Dollars' rebrand forced on under-25 undergrads
Vending machines and concessions now accept Dawg Dollars as workaround
key_quotes: Next step: ditch Aramark. They are terrible.
Yeah they made like 4 scummy changes to payments and shit this semester. Whoever the new treasury group is needs to be fired.
I get crazy headaches from not eating all day... Can anyone tell me which vending machines accept dawg dollars?

key_points: Homesickness is a real documented issue for freshmen
Student community is genuinely supportive in helping each other cope
No specific mental health resource complaints, but also little praise
key_quotes: I'm a freshman and I moved in on Thursday, but today is when I felt really homesick. I started thinking about my family and my friends at home and I was just really overwhelmed.
Stay busy. I mean busy, busy. Overwhelm yourself to the point where you can just barely do it all.
If you are looking to connect with other students, I would highly recommend applying to Freshman Reach.

key_points: Out-of-state tuition (~$48k) widely considered not worth it without aid
Merit scholarships negotiable if you push back with competing offers
New $450/credit overload fee hits students taking more than 16 credits
key_quotes: No public university is worth its out of state rate. Only consider going if you can get it waived to in-state.
I emailed Mississippi asking if there was anymore scholarship money they could offer, as Iowa State offered me a full tuition scholarship. They called me back.
Feels like I'm being taxed just trying to get an education.

key_points: Agriculture, wildlife, and natural resources programs are genuinely strong
Online psychology program praised for professor accessibility and LOR support
Engineering honors college exists; TA vs professor ratio unclear to students
key_quotes: I majored in Wildlife, Fisheries and Aquaculture... I loved all my wildlife classes, the professors are great and really want you to succeed.
I am finishing my BS in Psychology and have had the best experience with professors that made the last two years so fun.
At Mississippi State University - Bagley College of Engineering, do most students find their classes being taught by professors or TAs?

key_points: Parking director responded to student inquiry with literal 'no comment'
Serial filming incident in CS department mishandled for months by officials
Remote work policy contradicts itself; used as perk only when convenient
key_quotes: The university has no comment regarding your questions and observations.
I emailed the parking guy directly. Dismissive, rude, and arrogant is really all he is. They don't care.
So remote work is only a 'perk' when it benefits the university, never the employees. I'd expect nothing less from this school.

key_points: Starkville is genuinely small; Jackson is 2 hours away, Atlanta 3+
Campus is walkable and bikeable; outdoor recreation within 30 minutes
Cotton District is a beloved local area with good food and bars
key_quotes: My school is located in the middle of nowhere and is by no means close to any business or tech hubs. The closest big city is Jackson which is 3 hours away.
Starkville is SO easy to bike around, yall. No need to drive to campus if you're able to bike.
Campus looked gorgeous this weekend in general.

key_points: Location hurts recruiting; no major business hub nearby limits internships
Strong agriculture/wildlife alumni networks but finance/tech pipelines are weak
Full ride here beats debt at a more prestigious school per grad advice
key_quotes: I graduated HS and went to a pretty non-competitive undergraduate school... because that's where I got the full ride. I went to this school and it was 100% the right move.
I worked with a dentist who graduated from Mississippi State, nicest guy in the world that absolutely loved his school. Money talks and a full ride is nothing to pass up.
Does the location of your university matter for employment opportunities? I have no desire to stay in MS or in this area after graduation.

key_points: Free ride or deep in-state discount makes MSU genuinely excellent value
Out-of-state without major scholarship is hard to justify financially
New fees (overload credits, Dawg Dollars, parking) are eroding value fast
key_quotes: 72k vs. 0k. That alone kinda answers it. Undergrad is what you make of it.
Paying out of state for an Education degree is insane.
This is incredibly stupid. The people making these decisions are quite out of touch with the reality for most students.

key_points: Strong Southern football culture; cowbell identity is real and beloved
Community feel is warm but administration is increasingly tone-deaf
Students notice a steady erosion of traditions and student-friendly policies
key_quotes: I love this school. Just in general but I love its agriculture and wildlife programs too.
Too bad it's only taken Starkville 125 or so years to realize the benefits of partnering with State.
This is a reoccurring theme. It always gets worse. I feel bad for the freshmen because they don't have it as good as I had it.

key_points: **Safety:** Serial filmer in CS harassed women for months; admin sat on hands
**Parking:** Oversold permits, students told to 'pray they don't get a ticket'
**Administration:** Art Briles spotted at team practices; ethics concerns ignored
key_quotes: The school and especially the dean of students should be ashamed! The serial filmer is still an enrolled student here.
MSU has drastically changed their parking system this year to accommodate less than 2/3 as many students without any plans of developing more parking infrastructure.
As soon as Lebby was hired the admin made it clear they were okay with this as long as they are winning.

key_points: Outdoor recreation within 30 min: kayaking, fishing, camping at Choctaw Lake
Wildlife and natural resources faculty genuinely invested in student success
Ice storm weeks become legendary bonding experiences students remember forever
key_quotes: Snow/Ice weeks in Starkville are weeks you'll remember for the rest of your life. Stock up on groceries, get some alcohol if you drink, and have fun with your friends.
I'm currently a zookeeper! I loved all my wildlife classes, the professors are great and really want you to succeed.
If you stay and the weather gets really bad remember Starkville is still a small town. People will help.
